channel.title: My Portrait
channel.description: Updates for My Portrait — a private, on-device AI memory system for macOS.

item.description:
Features

  • Sharper on-screen text reading — A new OCR accuracy booster in Settings captures at full Retina resolution so text on screen is read more accurately, while your saved snapshots stay the same size on disk.
  • Keep audio until it's transcribed — A new Storage toggle (on by default) keeps audio that hasn't been transcribed yet past its delete-by date, so a transcription backlog can't silently erase text you wanted to keep.
  • Real, moving progress bars for Run now — Triggering event processing, portrait distillation, or personality refresh now shows a determinate progress bar with a percentage and the current step that glides through each stage, instead of a vague spinner that looks hung.
  • Writing-capture controls moved to Typing Capture — The writing-capture controls now live under Settings → Typing Capture, next to the data they act on; writing-style settings stay on the Memory page.

Bug fixes

  • Your prompts no longer leak to a temp file — Your messages, screen text, and personal memories are no longer written in plain text to a temp file that other apps on your Mac could read.
  • "Delete last 15m/30m/1h" now erases everything — Deleting recent activity also removes the screenshots, recordings, and video on disk, and clears audio that hadn't been transcribed yet, so deleted content can't come back as text later.
  • Settings changed right before quitting now save — A toggle you flip in the last moment before closing the app is saved to disk instead of silently disappearing on the next launch.
  • Fixed a startup crash on macOS 26 (Tahoe) — The app no longer randomly crashes on launch on macOS 26.
  • Quitting during audio transcription no longer crashes — Exiting while the app is catching up on a backlog of audio transcriptions no longer crashes it.
  • No audio lost when you pause or stop capture — The final segment you were speaking is saved and transcribed when capture stops, and turning transcription off temporarily keeps recordings waiting in the queue instead of marking them done with no text.
  • Old audio is cleaned up on schedule — With transcription turned off, old audio is now deleted on schedule instead of piling up and quietly filling your disk.
  • Speakerphone calls no longer transcribe twice — When a call plays through your speaker, your mic and the call audio used to both pick it up and produce two copies of the same words; duplicate transcripts across channels are now detected and merged into one.
  • Capture survives sleep, power changes, and quick toggles — After toggling capture off and on, the app keeps tracking screen sleep/wake; plugging or unplugging power reliably adjusts recording and transcription; and rapid on/off toggles run in order instead of racing.
  • Audio no longer garbles under heavy load — Recorded audio stays in order under heavy load, and rapidly toggling system-audio capture no longer spins up a duplicate stream.
  • Typing in Settings no longer interrupts recording — Changing an unrelated setting, or typing in a text field, no longer restarts the microphone and punches a gap in your recording.
  • No surprise Accessibility prompts, and voice training restores your mic — Changing a capture setting no longer triggers an unexpected Accessibility permission prompt, and a mic paused for voice training reliably turns back on afterward, even if you close the window or the app crashes mid-training.
  • Chat reply no longer vanishes mid-answer — Pressing Enter while the assistant is still typing no longer swallows the next reply into a blank bubble; your text stays put until the answer finishes.
  • Chat replies no longer lose their tail end — The last bit of a reply is no longer cut off, and continuing a conversation no longer silently breaks.
  • Switching conversations no longer gets stuck — Switching chats mid-answer no longer leaves a conversation stuck loading, and a reply you sent can no longer land in the wrong conversation if you switch away while it's preparing.
  • Claude Code detection is more reliable — My Portrait finds your Claude Code install even when your PATH is set up by nvm, asdf, or mise, and it re-checks a few minutes after a miss instead of staying blind until you restart.
  • Speaker edits and privacy deletes always save — Renaming or merging a speaker, saving voice training, or clearing memories before/after a date no longer silently fails when capture is busy writing.
  • Memory building is much more reliable — Building your daily memories now recovers from AI timeouts, crashes, sleep/wake, and partial failures instead of jamming all scheduled processing or mixing data between days, and the retry time it shows you is honest.
  • Stop actually stops a running pipeline — Hitting Stop on a memory run now kills every running AI process and aborts the remaining days, so a stopped pipeline doesn't keep churning in the background.
  • Rejecting a memory run behaves correctly — Turning down a finished run no longer permanently loses its output, greys out the Run button, or quietly shrinks your portrait, and a failed run resets itself so you can retry right away.
  • Portrait building stops failing silently — When the AI hits a snag while distilling your portrait, it recovers from messy model output and shows the real reason instead of looping forever or stalling with a vague error.
  • The latest day of activity gets remembered on time — Your most recent day, and the first day back after a break, now enter memory processing on the right date instead of being shifted a day off and skipped.
  • Memory files land in the right place — File names the AI generates that accidentally contain folder paths are cleaned up, so memories aren't scattered into stray sub-folders or duplicated.
  • Scheduled-task notifications come out clean — Notifications from your scheduled AI tasks no longer fire blank or garbled; the app reads only the real Notify section and ignores examples written inside code blocks.
  • The captured app matches the screen — When you switch apps quickly, a slow-loading icon for the previous app no longer overwrites the icon of the app you're actually looking at in the Timeline.
  • Fast day-switching shows the right day — Quickly jumping between days no longer leaves you looking at yesterday's screen while the date header says today.
  • Audio rows stop flickering in the Timeline — Repeated identical transcript snippets in the same clip no longer collide, so the audio list refreshes smoothly instead of glitching.
  • Captured-writing list shows the right group — Quickly clicking between captured-writing groups no longer shows one group's title over another group's entries.
  • Storage screen shows real numbers — The Storage page adds up the folders the app actually uses, so Audio, Video, and Screenshots show real sizes instead of a blank dash, plus a new Cache line for re-downloadable runtime and models.
  • Storage cleans up after itself — Captured snapshots and audio clean up more reliably: missing or broken files no longer leave orphaned data behind or jam the compaction queue, and an interrupted video compression no longer leaves a half-written file eating disk space.
  • Re-importing recovers missing screen history — If an import is interrupted partway, running it again now brings in the rest of your screen history instead of silently importing nothing.
  • AI can search your transcripts — Asking the AI to search spoken audio by keyword or speaker now actually finds matches instead of coming up empty, and re-importing your Screenpipe data no longer duplicates every transcript.
  • Email connection no longer hangs forever — When an email server is unreachable or refuses the connection, the Connections spinner fails cleanly instead of spinning forever and forcing a restart; a non-email test recipient is caught before any connection is made.
  • Setup picks a connected AI provider — During onboarding, the memory provider defaults to one you've actually connected instead of quietly selecting an unconnected one, and browsing provider options no longer wipes a custom model name you typed in.
  • Update settings edited by hand take effect immediately — If you change auto-update settings by editing the config file directly, the app picks up the change right away instead of needing a restart.
  • Faster, lighter transcription — Transcription loads its large model far less often (already-downloaded models skip re-initialization, and clips are batched), so it uses less memory and churns less in the background.
  • Smoother, lighter chat — Sending a message starts faster, the conversation no longer re-renders on every word as a reply streams in, and the app stops spinning a CPU core in the background after an answer finishes.
  • Snappier Memory pages — The Memory settings and Memories pages stay responsive while a pipeline runs, instead of hitting the database and disk on every redraw.
  • Faster timeline and speaker list — Opening your speakers and scrubbing the timeline are quicker, especially after a lot of recording has piled up.
  • Lighter background screen capture — Screen capture skips an expensive per-frame window scan when you have no privacy-mask rules set, doing less work on every captured frame.
  • Imports no longer stall live capture — Importing screen history now commits in small batches, so your live screen and audio capture keep running smoothly instead of freezing for minutes during a big import.

Features

  • Automatic or manual import scanning — A new "Auto-scan everything from imports" toggle in General lets the Import page wait until you press Scan on each source, instead of scanning screenpipe / Claude Code / Codex automatically every time you open it.
  • Collapse the RECENTS list — The sidebar's RECENTS section now collapses with a chevron, and the New chat button stays available even when it's collapsed.
  • Right-click menus in Memories — Right-click a folder to rename, recolor, or delete it; right-click an event to move it into a folder (or a new one) or delete it. The item you right-click is highlighted so it's clear which one the menu applies to.
  • Balanced power mode — You can now pick Balanced directly (between Performance and Battery saver), and Auto shows the tier it's currently running, e.g. "Auto — Balanced".
  • Simpler scheduler control — The Memory scheduler now has a single Auto-run on/off toggle instead of the Frequency / Time / Day pickers, which didn't actually change when pipelines ran.

Bug fixes

  • Claude Code and Codex scan independently
  • Folder color swatches show their real colors — The Change color menu used to render every swatch gray; each now shows its actual color, and the default is labeled "Default (random)".
  • CRON JOB HISTORY collapse header — The header is now clickable across the whole row, and toggling it no longer changes the card height or nudges the content below.
  • Voice training can be retried after a failure — A failed attempt (such as the too-short recording warning) no longer leaves the card stuck on a stale error with a disabled Start button.
  • Stuck transcription queue clears itself — An audio clip whose file went missing no longer jams the queue forever at "1 clip in queue · Transcribing…".
  • AI model pickers updated — The OpenAI (your-own-key) and Gemini model lists now offer current 2026 models instead of retired ones that failed when selected.
  • Divider lines visible in Light mode — Separator lines across Settings, chat, Connections, Cron Jobs, Timeline, and Onboarding no longer disappear on a light background.
  • More accurate writing capture

Features

  • Compact tool blocks in chat — A reply's thinking and tool steps collapse into one expandable summary bar; long chats load faster. Click to expand the details. Toggle in Settings → Display → Chat.
  • Dismiss a stuck "Needs attention" day — You can accept a day as-is so it stops being flagged.

Bug fixes

  • Cleaner chat copy — The copy button now copies only the assistant's final answer, not its thinking or the commands it ran.
  • Higher writing capture accuracy
  • "Needs attention" clears after a successful retry — The row no longer lingers once the day reprocesses.
  • "Needs attention" retries promptly — Retry now skips the once-a-day guard instead of staying stuck for ~9 hours.

Features

  • Better bug reporting — When something goes wrong, the diagnostic bundle (Settings → Health → "Export diagnostic bundle") now includes crash and out-of-memory reports, and exporting one walks you through filing a bug report — complete with a GitHub issue template that asks for the bundle.

Bug fixes

  • Fixed an audio-capture crash — Rapidly toggling Audio Capture on/off, or a Bluetooth mic switching mid-session, no longer crashes the app.
  • No more empty Follow-up Reminders notifications — When there's nothing urgent to flag, the reminder stays quiet instead of sending a blank notification.
  • Cleaner wording — Tidied the empty-state text for portrait categories, and the diagnostic export note now correctly says Downloads (not Desktop).

Features

  • The assistant reads your captured data reliably — Ask about a meeting, your screen history, or what you've been working on, and the in-app assistant now consistently pulls the real data to answer — the full meeting transcript (not just the first few minutes), and it knows all of its data tools on every turn instead of forgetting after the first message.
  • Pipeline run history — The Changelog page now shows your last 50 memory-pipeline runs (Run now and scheduled, with success / failure / auto-recovering outcomes), instead of raw portrait edits.
  • Faster memory processing — Event building now uses a tighter context window, cutting per-day processing time and AI usage.

Bug fixes

  • Incognito windows stay private — A visible incognito / private browser window is never recorded now — even a small one off to the side, on a second monitor, or when it isn't the focused window.
  • No false "capture stalled" while incognito is open — Keeping an incognito window open no longer trips a false health alert.
  • The captured app matches the screen — When you switch apps, the app and icon recorded for that moment now match what was actually on screen, instead of briefly showing the previous app.
  • "Check for updates" always responds — Clicking Check now while an update is already downloading no longer does nothing; it now tells you the update is in progress.
  • Memory runs report their outcome accurately — A partly-failed memory run is no longer reported as a success.
  • No stale "interrupted" alert on launch — The memory scheduler stopped showing a leftover "interrupted" notification every time the app starts.
  • Writing capture accuracy — Pasted and placeholder text no longer leaks into your captured writing, and canvas capture now drops on-screen content you didn't actually type.

Features

  • Switchable speaker‑ID model — Choose between English and Chinese voice models in Audio Capture → Speakers. The Chinese models are far more accurate at telling Chinese speakers apart. Each trained voice is tied to its model, so switching back and forth never loses your enrollments.
  • Smart re‑grouping after you name a speaker — Name or merge a detected voice and today's audio is re‑scanned automatically, so that person's other clips get pulled together.
  • Memory pipeline notifications — Optional notifications when each memory pipeline finishes, plus a separate alert if the app had to restart after an interruption.
  • "Needs attention" for memory processing — When a memory step can't finish on its own it now surfaces clearly with the reason, and keeps retrying with long backoff instead of silently giving up.
  • Delete a folder — Remove a memory folder (ungroups its events) without deleting anything.
  • Auto‑scroll to who's talking — Scrubbing the timeline now scrolls the matching transcript line into view.

Bug fixes

  • Bluetooth call audio now records — System audio capture moved to ScreenCaptureKit, so the other side of a call is captured even on Bluetooth headphones.
  • Better speaker separation — Voice matching no longer over‑splits one person into many, and "Merge duplicates" actually merges now.
  • No more false "screen capture frozen" when the window is covered by another app.
  • Timeline stays put — Background refreshes don't yank you to the newest frame, and switching frames no longer flickers.
  • Steadier memory scheduler — Accurate "Running" status, better stuck‑step detection, and clearer failure reasons.
  • Folder list polish — Cleaner separators and a reliable folder delete button.
  • Better writing capture algorithm and higher accuracy

Features

  • Live progress for Memory jobs — each manual run now shows the stage it's on (building events, scoring, analyzing your writing, …), and every job's staged results collect in one Pending review area.
  • Stop one job, not all of them — when several Memory jobs run at once, Stop now only stops the one you picked, and never touches your chats.
  • Follow-up Reminders now remembers items still open from previous runs and follows up on them, instead of starting fresh each time.
  • Speakers settings: the old AI "Organize" is now a simpler Merge duplicates.
  • A new Open ~/.portrait shortcut in Settings, plus a tidier sidebar order.

Bug fixes

  • Pasted text no longer leaks stray placeholder characters into your captured writing.
  • Fixed the last character of pinyin / IME input occasionally getting dropped.
  • More reliable detection of when you've finished and sent a message.
  • Memory's Needs attention list now shows plain-language status instead of internal codes.
  • Fixed several Settings descriptions that didn't match what the control actually does, and trimmed a lot of jargon.

Features

  • Keep Mac awake while transcribing — On AC power, My Portrait can keep your Mac from idle-sleeping while there's a transcription backlog, so it finishes instead of dozing off. New toggle in Audio Capture (off by default).

Bug fixes

  • Microphone selection now works — Picking a specific input device (instead of "Follow system default") now actually records from that mic.
  • Audio Capture fully releases the mic when turned off — No more lingering "microphone in use"; system-audio capture stops with it.
  • Mic no longer gets stuck on after toggling Audio Capture off.
  • Faster transcription catch-up — A large backlog now transcribes continuously instead of a couple of clips per minute.
  • Memory freed after transcribing — The on-device model fully releases its memory once the queue empties.
  • Better speaker recognition — Fewer mix-ups between similar voices; uncertain segments are left unlabeled instead of mislabeled.
  • "Organize speakers with AI" works without an AI provider — Merging duplicate voice clusters no longer fails when no AI is configured.
  • Input-device picker no longer flickers while audio capture starts.
  • "Only transcribe while plugged in" applies instantly instead of after a delay.
  • Fixed a false "transcription backlog stalled" health alert during normal catch-up.
  • Transcription memory usage is now reported accurately.

Features

  • Image lightbox — click an image in chat to view it full-screen.
  • AI folder organization — the assistant can now tidy your memory folders (new mp-folders tool + skill).
  • Per-engine transcription languages — each transcription engine keeps its own language selection; switching engines no longer wipes it.
  • Editable "pause capture" list — pick which apps/sites pause screen recording (Netflix, Disney+, etc.), right in Screen Capture settings.
  • Live transcription status — Audio Capture settings now show the transcription queue and memory use in real time.
  • Tunable paste capture — control how clipboard pastes are kept (or dropped) in writing capture.

Bug fixes

  • Fixed a chat hang — the assistant could occasionally get stuck on "thinking…" forever.
  • Stopped deletion of your original Screenpipe recordings — imported audio is now copied into the app's own data, and cleanup never touches read-only Screenpipe files.
  • "Delete recent data" now actually deletes — it was silently failing to remove transcripts and frames.
  • Fixed a memory blow-up during live transcription (Qwen/MLX) that could exhaust RAM.
  • Graceful quit fixed — shutdown cleanup (flush to disk, close capture) now actually runs when you quit.
  • DRM detection no longer freezes screen capture — recording resumes correctly after protected video.
  • Microphone picker — fixed phantom checkmarks (now a native picker).
  • AI setup no longer hangs while installing the Pi agent.
  • Many smaller crash, data-race, memory, and correctness fixes across capture, memory, and writing capture.

Features

  • New on-device Qwen3-ASR transcription engine — better Chinese, no extra setup. Download models on demand.
  • Faster & lighter — removed the memory-heavy semantic search; search now runs on FTS5.
  • Pick your microphone (or follow the system default) with auto-fallback when devices unplug, plus a live "recording from" indicator.
  • Reliable system-audio capture — records the other side of calls and what you hear, even after switching output devices.
  • Pause capture for chosen apps or categories while they're playing audio (configurable, not just music).
  • Voice ID, reworked — train your voice (and others), listen to a clip of any detected voice, and auto-organize clusters. Trained speakers are searchable as @speaker:<name> in chat.
  • Auto-organize events into project folders (Event classifier) on a schedule.
  • Import conversations from Claude Code CLI and Codex CLI.
  • Manage scheduled jobs from chat — create, list, and delete them; live streaming; per-job mute; drag-and-drop & paste images.
  • Health & diagnostics — stall detection in the menu bar + one-click diagnostic export.
  • Multi-language transcription picker and "only transcribe while plugged in".

Bug fixes

  • System audio no longer goes silent shortly after recording starts.
  • Fixed an occasional app freeze caused by a database deadlock.
  • ChatGPT login no longer breaks under concurrent token refresh.
  • Crash fixes in the audio-capture restart path and typing capture.
  • Writing capture is more verbatim and accurate.
  • Light-theme visibility fixes across chat and connection views.
  • Removed a batch of dead or misleading settings.
  • Privacy controls: ignore incognito windows, record while screen is locked, and Power mode.

Features

  • Browser & canvas writing capture — properly captures what you write in Safari/Chrome/Arc and canvas editors (Google Docs, Notion), reconstructing edit history to match the document's own version history.
  • Ask the AI about what you wrote — chat now answers from your distilled writing records, and search returns the exact matching frame with a snippet.
  • Paste & drag-and-drop images and files straight into the chat input.
  • Daily scheduled jobs catch up — if the app was closed at a job's scheduled time, it runs on next launch instead of waiting another 24 hours.

Bug fixes

  • Toggling system audio off no longer crashes the app.
  • Writing capture is more accurate — keystroke-gated, attributed per browser tab, with far fewer false positives.

Features

  • Chat now knows what you've been doing — ask "summarize my yesterday" or "what was I writing in Cursor" and it answers from your real screen activity, all queried on-device.
  • Mode-aware Quick Actions — the suggestion cards adapt to whether you've been coding, browsing, in a meeting, or writing, and send the relevant context along.
  • Pre-downloaded transcription models — all 4 Whisper sizes ship on first launch, so switching size is instant.
  • Local capture models card shows ready / downloading status for each on-device model.

Bug fixes

  • Voice training works — fixed three crashes and two correctness bugs; re-training a name now replaces the old samples.
  • Time accounting now matches macOS Screen Time (was off by ~7×).
  • Chat input grows to 4 lines then scrolls; hovering a message no longer shifts it, and copy/edit/regenerate work from anywhere on the bubble.
  • Light-theme fixes for the timeline toolbar/calendar and the Memory/Input middle column.

Bug fixes

  • Voice training no longer crashes when you click "Start training".
  • The Memory page's middle column now matches the Light theme (no more out-of-place dark grey panel).

Bug fixes

  • Light theme: chat input, the @ / shield / paperclip icons, and the setup banner are readable again (were white-on-white).
  • Light theme: timeline calendar dates, month switcher and selected-day highlight are visible again.
  • Light theme: Connections buttons, the Memory search bar, and Settings tab icons / range chips now adapt correctly.

Features

  • Voice training, rebuilt — fully decoupled from transcription (record 30s on-device, no power needed); re-training replaces the old samples; it pre-checks the voice model is downloaded before recording.
  • Cleaner writing capture — strips window chrome, far fewer duplicates, no truncation of long writing, better per-record summaries, and keystroke-gated so it's signal-only.
  • Local capture models card in Settings → AI models shows ready / downloading status for each on-device model.

Bug fixes

  • Toggling system audio no longer crashes on an audio-format mismatch.
  • Notification overlay: Light-theme colors fixed, and the panel height now matches its content.

Features

  • Fully silent auto-update — new versions download in the background, show a small "click to postpone" banner, and install on their own after 10 seconds.
  • The update-check interval now respects your setting, and "Check now" always gives an explicit yes/no.
  • The installed version now shows in Settings → General and the menu-bar dropdown.

Bug fixes

  • Notification overlay panel resized to a sane height.

Features

  • Import your existing Screenpipe data — auto-scans disks and imports MP4 / audio metadata, with a progress bar.
  • Local models pre-fetch on launch, so the first run no longer stalls.
  • Proper Light theme — warm-white → lavender gradient with dynamic glass.
  • Custom dock icon, auto-cropped to the macOS squircle.
  • Speakers folded into the Audio Capture page.

Bug fixes

  • Chat: provider/model locked per conversation; RECENTS ordering fixed; empty placeholder hidden.
  • Onboarding no longer proactively requests permissions; window-resize fix.
  • Scheduler: 24-hour time picker with aligned controls.
  • Connections: brand icons for DeepSeek / Ollama / Anthropic / Obsidian / Notion; Claude Code "Connect" actually verifies the link.
  • Cron jobs: trimmed conversations no longer leak into RECENTS.
  • Sidebar: Memories rows fully clickable; translucent-sidebar toggle takes effect.
  • Assorted icon, layout, and Swift 6 data-race fixes.

⚠️ Upgrading from 1.0.0: install this .dmg manually and re-grant Screen Recording / Microphone / Accessibility / Input Monitoring / Full Disk Access — the code-signing identity changed, so this one update can't go through Sparkle and macOS treats it as a new app. Auto-updates work normally after 1.0.81.
• First release — see the GitHub release page for full notes.
